LAW AND MOTION, 301, THE COURT DENIES DEFENDANTS PACIFIC UNION REAL ESTATE GROUP, DEBBIE HEMINGWAY AND KATHLEEN TAGGART'S MOTION CONTESTING GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT. THE COURT TENTATIVELY APPROVES THE GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT FOR A TOTAL OF $217,000; HOWEVER, THE COURT CANNOT APPROVE THE GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IF IT INCLUDES $20,000 ALLOCATED TO BODILY INJURY OR EMOTIONAL DISTRESS; THE COURT FINDS THERE IS NO BASIS FOR BODILY INJURY DAMAGES. STIPULATION WILL BE PRESENTED TO THE COURT; IT NEED NOT BE APPROVED BY DEFENDANTS PACIFIC UNION REAL ESTATE, TAGGART AND HEMINGWAY'S COUNSEL. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558

MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT PETER NAUGHTON, NAUGHTON REALTY, INC. A CALIF. CORP. MOTION Joinder Contesting Good Faith Settlement - DUPLICATE ENTRY.

Hearing On Motion Contesting Good Faith Settlement, Moving Parties, Defendants Debbie Hemingway, Kathleen Taggart, Pacific Union Real Estate Group, Ltd.
Off Calendar Dec-05-2003 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Shannon Jones, Esq., (925) 837-2715 for Moving Party Defendants Pacific Union, Kathleen Taggart and Debbie Hemingway Motion Contesting Good Faith Settlement
Susan A. Bush, Esq., Wild, Carey & Fife (415) 837-3101 for Defendant Peter Naughton, Naughton Realty, Inc. Joinder to Defendants Pacific Union, Taggart and Hemingway's Motion
Sharon Hightower, Esq., Ericksen, Arbuthnot, Kilduff, Day & Lindstrom, Inc. (408) 286-0880, for Opposing Party Defendants Bill Kaul dba Bill Kaul Inspection Services
Bryan Sean McKown, Esq., Bolfango & McKown LLP (415) 398-7390 for Opposing Party Plaintiffs Paul Hernandez and Lauren Hernandez
RULING:
A R G U E D; The Court DENIES Defendants Pacific Union Real Estate Group, Debbie Hemingway and Kathleen Taggart's MOTION CONTESTING GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT. The Court tentatively approves the Good Faith Settlement for a total of $217,000; however, the Court cannot approve the Good Faith Settlement if it includes $20,000 allocated to bodily injury or emotional distress; the Court finds there is no basis for bodily injury damages. Stipulation will be presented to the Court; it need not be approved by Defendants Pacific Union Real Estate, Taggart and Hemingway's Counsel. (301/JLW/JT) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
